steps.thought: Which action maximizes my probability of winning this round? It’s round 1 of 5, so I need to balance immediate payoff against inducing the opponent’s future behavior. In Prisoner’s Dilemma, a first-round cooperate is often best against a reciprocal or mistake-prone opponent because it can establish mutual cooperation and keep future scores high. Since a single defection may just be an error, starting with cooperation gives the best chance to explore cooperation without prematurely escalating to mutual defection. I’ll take the cooperative opening.
